title: Backblaze has stopped backing up OneDrive and Dropbox folders and maybe others
author: rrreese
aiHeadline: Backblaze 'innovates' by stopping backup services for OneDrive and Dropbox, proving that sometimes the best way to 'improve' is to just stop doing things altogether.

title: A new spam policy for “back button hijacking”
author: zdw
aiHeadline: Google cracks down on 'back button hijacking,' proving that even the most obscure internet annoyances can become the next big thing in web policy enforcement.

title: Rare concert recordings are landing on the Internet Archive
author: jrm-veris
aiHeadline: Thousands of rare concert recordings find their way to the Internet Archive, proving that 'rare' is just a temporary state before the internet finds it and makes it freely available to everyone.

title: I wrote to Flock's privacy contact to opt out of their domestic spying program
author: speckx
aiHeadline: One person's attempt to opt out of Flock's domestic spying program, proving that sometimes the best way to handle privacy is to just write a blog post about it and hope for the best.

title: jj – the CLI for Jujutsu
author: tigerlily
aiHeadline: jj: The CLI for Jujutsu, because who doesn't want their command-line tools to feel as confusing and mystical as a martial art?
